en.extract:
The Tanimbar monarch (Carterornis castus), or Loetoe monarch is a bird in the family Monarchidae endemic to Indonesia. It is found in the Tanimbar Islands.


Taxonomy and systematics
This subspecies was formerly placed in the genus Monarcha until moved to Carterornis in 2009. While the Tanimbar monarch was formerly considered a subspecies of the white-naped monarch (C. pileatus), it is now considered a separate species.

The '''Tanimbar monarch''' ('''''Carterornis castus'''''), or '''Loetoe monarch''' is a [[bird]] in the family [[Monarchidae]] [[Endemism|endemic]] to Indonesia. It is found in the [[Tanimbar Islands]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==
This subspecies was formerly placed in the genus ''[[Monarcha]]'' until moved to ''[[Carterornis]]'' in 2009.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/Archives/Master_IOC_list_v2.0.xls|title=IOC Bird List v2.0|last=|first=|date=2009|website=|publisher=|access-date=October 28, 2016}}</ref> While the Tanimbar monarch was formerly considered a subspecies of the [[white-naped monarch]] (''C. pileatus''), it is now considered a separate species.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=640B813A&sec=summary&ssver=1|title=Carterornis castus - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2017-01-21}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Species Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/species-updates/|access-date=2021-06-04|language=en-US}}</ref>
